name: Extensis Font and Digital Asset Management API
what_it_does: Extensis digital asset and font management REST API for creative teams and agencies to manage font libraries, activate/deactivate fonts, and organize digital assets with integration into Adobe Creative Cloud applications. Enables AI agents to manage font library organization and metadata tagging for creative asset automation, handle font activation and deactivation for design workflow automation, access font search and discovery for typography selection automation, retrieve font license tracking and compliance monitoring for font rights management automation, manage Portfolio digital asset management for creative asset organization automation, handle asset collection and category management for DAM taxonomy automation, access Google Fonts and Adobe Fonts integration for web font management automation, retrieve font analytics and usage tracking for license compliance automation, manage team font access and permission for creative team governance automation, and integrate Extensis with Adobe InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop, and Creative Cloud for in-app font activation automation.
use_cases: Managing font libraries from creative team font governance agents
Activating fonts from design workflow automation agents
Tracking font licenses from compliance monitoring agents
Organizing digital assets from creative DAM agents
not_for: Full-featured DAM for marketing assets and brand management (use Brandfolder or Bynder)
Video and media production asset management (use Frame.io)
Web font delivery and CDN (use Google Fonts or Adobe Fonts directly)
best_when: A creative agency or design team wanting AI agents to automate font library management, font activation in Creative Cloud apps, license compliance tracking, and creative asset organization through Extensis's typography-focused creative management platform.
avoid_when: ADOBE PLUGIN DEPENDENCY FOR FONT ACTIVATION: Extensis font activation in Adobe apps requires the Extensis plugin installed locally; automated font activation via API without plugin creates fonts available in Extensis library but not activated in Adobe applications; automated creative workflow that assumes API font activation immediately available in InDesign/Illustrator requires plugin presence verification. FONT LICENSE COMPLIANCE COMPLEXITY FOR AUTOMATED USAGE: Font licenses have complex usage rights (desktop, web, app, print); automated font deployment must validate license scope against intended use case; automated font selection by design workflow that uses desktop-licensed fonts in web deployment creates license violation; implement license scope validation in automated font selection. PORTFOLIO DAM vs FONT MANAGEMENT AS SEPARATE PRODUCTS: Extensis Portfolio (DAM) and Suitcase Fusion/Universal Type Server (font management) are separate product lines with potentially separate APIs; automated creative workflows integrating both products must verify unified API availability; some integrations may require separate credentials per product line.

auth.notes: Extensis uses API key authentication. REST API with JSON. Portland, Oregon HQ. Founded 1993. Private, PE-backed (Accel-KKR). Products: Portfolio (DAM), Suitcase Fusion (desktop font manager), Universal Type Server (team font server), Connect (Adobe plugin). Adobe Creative Cloud plugin integration. Font management for creative teams and agencies. GDPR. Competes with FontExplorer, Adobe Fonts, and Monotype for font management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — 30-day trial for Portfolio DAM
pricing.paid_tiers: Portfolio DAM: per-user annual; Universal Type Server: per-server license; Suitcase Fusion: per-user; custom enterprise pricing
pricing.notes: Portland OR. Accel-KKR PE backed. Per-user annual subscription. 30-day trial. Enterprise licensing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; font and asset queries throttled; no webhooks — polling for library changes

known_agent_gotchas: FONT ACTIVATION REQUIRES LOCAL PLUGIN — NOT PURE API: Extensis font activation in design apps requires the Connect plugin installed on each designer's machine; automated font activation via API marks font as active in Extensis cloud but requires plugin sync to activate locally in Adobe apps; automated font activation without plugin synchronization creates mismatch between Extensis state and local availability

UNIVERSAL TYPE SERVER vs SUITCASE vs PORTFOLIO API SCOPE: Extensis has multiple products (Portfolio DAM, Suitcase Fusion, Universal Type Server) with potentially different API scopes and endpoints; automated creative workflow must verify correct product API for target functionality; cross-product automation may require multiple API integrations rather than single unified API

NO WEBHOOKS FOR FONT ACTIVATION CONFIRMATION: Extensis does not provide webhooks for font activation events; automated font availability verification must poll for activation status; automated design workflow that proceeds immediately after activation API call without polling verification may use not-yet-activated fonts

FONT LICENSE FIELD METADATA RELIABILITY: Extensis font library license metadata depends on correct license information at import time; automated license compliance checking via API relies on accurate license metadata; automated license validation for fonts imported without proper license metadata creates false compliance reporting

PORTFOLIO CATALOG vs FONT LIBRARY SEPARATE NAMESPACES: Extensis Portfolio (digital assets) and font library are separate organizational namespaces; automated search for creative assets must specify correct catalog type; automated search across both fonts and digital assets requires separate queries to font library and Portfolio catalog endpoints
agent_readiness.security_notes: Font and asset management. GDPR. API key. US. Font library, license, and creative asset data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Font management. GDPR. API key. US. Font library and creative asset data.
